name: Old Baths Trail Loop
name_original: Balneario de Ledesma desde Juzbado — circular por el Camino Viejo de los Baños
description: Hike a moderate 5.4-mile circular trail from Juzbado to Balneario de Ledesma, exploring dehesas, river views, and historical paths.
region_intro: Hiking around Juzbado offers routes through the distinctive *dehesa* landscape, characterized by open woodlands of oak and cork trees. The municipality is situated within the Tormes River valley, providing varied terrain and scenic views. Hikers can explore areas with significant botanical interest and geological features, including the Juzbado fault. The region's commitment to sustainable development preserves its natural charm for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails around Juzbado The most popular hiking route is Puente Mocho and Calzada Romana Trail, a 4.9 miles (7.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 41 minutes to complete. This easy route leads through the *dehesa* landscape to a historic Roman bridge. Another top favourite among local hikers is Old Baths Trail Loop, a moderate 5.4 miles (8.6 km) path. This trail offers views of the Tormes River valley and takes approximately 2 hours 20 minutes. Local hikers also love the Santa Lucía Trail Loop, a 7.3 miles (11.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 5 minutes. Hiking around Juzbado is defined by the Tormes River valley, *dehesa* landscapes, and viewpoints like Peña del Castillo. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate hikes. The routes in Juzbado are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from 7 reviews. More than 36 hikers have used komoot to explore Juzbado's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Juzbado?
A: Juzbado is known for its distinctive &lt;em&gt;dehesa&lt;/em&gt; landscape, characterized by open woodlands of oak and cork trees. You'll also find routes traversing the picturesque Tormes River valley, offering varied terrain and scenic views. The area also has geological significance, including the Juzbado fault.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, Juzbado offers several easy routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993164045/puente-mocho-and-calzada-romana-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Mocho and Calzada Romana Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.9-mile (7.8 km) path that leads through the &lt;em&gt;dehesa&lt;/em&gt; landscape to a historic Roman bridge. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31488762" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Torresmenudas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest to look out for on the trails?
A: Hikers can explore several interesting features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4859387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Mocho&lt;/a&gt;, a historic Roman bridge, is a key attraction. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5574008" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tormes River – Ledesma Bridge&lt;/a&gt; nearby, which has Roman origins. The Peña del Castillo promontory offers splendid panoramic views over the Tormes River valley.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Juzbado?
A: Yes, several routes in Juzbado are designed as loops. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1210322830/old-baths-trail-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Baths Trail Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.4-mile (8.6 km) path offering views of the Tormes River valley. Another popular circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1208816730/santa-lucia-trail-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Lucía Trail Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.3 miles (11.8 km) long.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Juzbado?
A: The routes in Juzbado are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from 7 reviews. Hikers often praise the serene &lt;em&gt;dehesa&lt;/em&gt; landscapes, the scenic views of the Tormes River valley, and the well-preserved natural and historical elements along the paths.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Juzbado suitable for families with children?
A: Many of the trails in Juzbado, particularly the easy-graded ones, are suitable for families. The varied terrain and natural features like the &lt;em&gt;dehesa&lt;/em&gt; and river views offer an engaging experience for children. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993164045/puente-mocho-and-calzada-romana-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Mocho and Calzada Romana Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a good option for a family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Juzbado?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on each trail may vary, the natural and open character of the &lt;em&gt;dehesa&lt;/em&gt; landscape generally makes it suitable for dog walking. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and ensure you clean up after them to preserve the natural environment.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Juzbado?
A: The region's Mediterranean climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for hiking, with milder temperatures and lush landscapes. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are preferable. Winter offers cooler, crisp conditions, though some paths might be muddy after rain.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes available for experienced hikers?
A: While Juzbado primarily features easy to moderate trails, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1208816730/santa-lucia-trail-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Lucía Trail Loop&lt;/a&gt;, at 7.3 miles (11.8 km), offers a longer moderate challenge. Its varied terrain and duration of approximately 3 hours 5 minutes provide a more extended experience for those seeking a longer walk.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads in Juzbado?
A: Juzbado is a small municipality, and while specific designated parking areas for every trailhead might not be explicitly listed, parking is generally available within or on the outskirts of the village, often near key attractions or starting points for popular routes. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking in Juzbado?
A: The &lt;em&gt;dehesa&lt;/em&gt; landscape and Tormes River valley are home to diverse wildlife. You might spot various bird species, including raptors, and smaller mammals typical of Mediterranean woodlands. The area's commitment to sustainable development helps preserve these natural habitats.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural elements integrated into the hiking experience?
A: Yes, Juzbado actively integrates culture and history with its natural landscapes. Beyond the Roman bridges like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4859387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Mocho&lt;/a&gt;, the area around the former San Miguel Hermitage holds botanical significance. The region also connects landscape with poetry, enriching the cultural aspect of your hike.
tips: A photogenic place overlooking the Tormes River on the Camino Viejo de los Baños between Juzbado and Baños de Ledesma.
The name of the town Baños de Ledesma comes from its famous hot springs, which we can enjoy in this spa.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The circular route from Juzbado to Balneario de Ledesma is rated as moderate, requiring good fitness and sure-footedness. While parts of the Camino Viejo de los Baños are generally low difficulty, other sections, especially on the return loop, present more demanding conditions. You'll encounter a variety of surfaces, including rural roads (caminos vecinales) and potentially rougher, uneven ground, with ascents and descents contributing to the moderate rating.

faqs.question: What natural features and viewpoints can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The trail offers rich and varied scenery. You'll traverse well-preserved dehesas, characteristic of Salamanca, and walk alongside the Tormes River. Look out for riparian forests, granite outcrops (cortados graníticos) overlooking the river, and spectacular panoramic views of the Tormes' meanders. Don't miss the Natural viewpoint for expansive vistas.

faqs.question: Does the trail have any historical or cultural significance?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Camino Viejo de los Baños follows the path of an ancient Roman road that connected Salamanca with Ledesma. The area around Juzbado also has evidence of human presence dating back to the 5th century BC. Juzbado itself is known as 'the town of poets,' with poetry integrated into the landscape and displayed in public spaces.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife or natural beauty can I observe?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the protected LIC (Lugar de Interés Comunitario) of the Tormes riverbanks and its tributaries, showcasing a rich array of Mediterranean forest ecosystems. It's an excellent spot for birdwatching, with opportunities to spot royal herons, booted eagles, kingfishers, and various types of ducks, depending on the season.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Juzbado?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for the trailhead in Juzbado are not provided, but as a circular route starting and ending in Juzbado, you should look for public parking options within the village. It's advisable to arrive early, especially on weekends, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, there are no explicit restrictions against dogs. However, as a general rule for protected areas and for the safety and comfort of other hikers and wildlife, it's recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through dehesas or near livestock.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during most seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural colors, making them ideal. Summer can be hot, so an early start is recommended. Winter hiking is possible, but be prepared for cooler temperatures and potentially muddy sections after rain.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail. It passes through the LIC Riberas del Rio Tormes y Afluentes, which is a protected area, but access for hiking is generally free. Please respect the natural environment and follow any local regulations.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, while primarily following the Camino Viejo de los Baños (61% of the route), this circular hike also briefly intersects with sections of the Ruta del Tormes: Variante 4-B (13%), Calle Río Tormes (11%), and smaller parts of Camino del Pocito (2%) and Camino de los Pilones (2%).

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Juzbado, the starting and ending point of the circular route, is a village where you may find some basic amenities like cafes or pubs. The route also passes by the Ledesma Spa, which offers facilities, but check their operating hours if you plan to visit.

faqs.question: Is this a suitable hike for families?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty rating, the circular route might be challenging for very young children or those unaccustomed to hiking. However, families with older children who have good fitness and sure-footedness could enjoy the diverse scenery and historical aspects. Consider the 8.6 km distance and 120m elevation gain when planning.
